PERF-001.description: Compiles a regular expression inside a Go loop body instead of compiling once and reusing the compiled pattern.
PERF-001.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match regexp.MustCompile or regexp.Compile inside loop bodies and suppress when compilation is hoisted outside the loop.
PERF-001.name: Regular Expression Compilation Inside Loop
PERF-001.original_description: Detect regexp.MustCompile(...) or regexp.Compile(...) inside a for or range loop body.

PERF-002.description: Repeatedly grows a string with concatenation inside a loop instead of using a builder, buffer, or batched join.
PERF-002.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match repeated string concatenation in loops and suppress when strings.Builder, bytes.Buffer, or equivalent batching is already used.
PERF-002.name: String Concatenation Inside Loop
PERF-002.original_description: Detect s = s + part or s += part inside a for or range loop body.

PERF-003.description: Rebuilds a working slice inside a loop in a way that repeatedly discards capacity or recreates the same accumulation buffer.
PERF-003.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match slice reset or make patterns inside loops and suppress when reuse via slicing or intentional one-shot allocation is evident.
PERF-003.name: Slice Rebuild Inside Loop
PERF-003.original_description: Detect loop-local slice re-creation followed by repeated append in the same loop body.

PERF-004.description: Allocates a new map inside a loop body where the map could be reused or hoisted to avoid repeated allocation churn.
PERF-004.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match make(map[...]) inside loop bodies and suppress when the allocation is intentional and not on a hot path.
PERF-004.name: Map Allocation Inside Loop
PERF-004.original_description: Detect make(map[K]V) inside a for or range loop body.

PERF-005.description: Performs repeated JSON marshal, unmarshal, encoder, or decoder setup inside an inner loop or row-by-row hot path.
PERF-005.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match repeated JSON conversion in hot paths and suppress when the code already batches work or reuses encoder or decoder state appropriately.
PERF-005.name: JSON Encode Or Decode In Hot Loop
PERF-005.original_description: Detect json.Marshal, json.Unmarshal, json.NewEncoder, or json.NewDecoder in a tight loop or repeated request-side hot path.

PERF-006.description: Uses fmt-based string formatting repeatedly inside a loop where direct writes, builders, or cached formatting would be cheaper.
PERF-006.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match fmt formatting in loop bodies and suppress when the code is already writing directly to a reusable buffer or writer.
PERF-006.name: fmt Formatting Inside Loop
PERF-006.original_description: Detect fmt.Sprintf or fmt.Fprintf used as repeated string construction in a loop.

PERF-007.description: Places defer statements inside loops, causing cleanup work to stack until function exit and adding avoidable per-iteration overhead.
PERF-007.detection_notes: Match a defer whose nearest enclosing loop executes in the same function scope. Suppress a defer inside a function literal declared by that loop, because it runs when each closure returns; still report a loop nested inside that closure.
PERF-007.name: Defer Inside Loop
PERF-007.original_description: Detect defer inside a for or range loop body.

PERF-008.description: Repeatedly parses time values in a loop using a constant layout on a hot path instead of restructuring or caching work.
PERF-008.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match repeated time parsing inside loops and suppress when the parse is outside the hot path or the loop is clearly small and bounded.
PERF-008.name: Time Parsing Inside Loop
PERF-008.original_description: Detect time.Parse or time.ParseInLocation inside loop bodies.

PERF-009.description: Repeatedly parses URLs inside a tight loop rather than moving parsing or validation to a cheaper boundary.
PERF-009.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match repeated URL parsing in loops and suppress when parsing happens on a cold path or only for a trivially small collection.
PERF-009.name: URL Parsing Inside Loop
PERF-009.original_description: Detect url.Parse or url.ParseRequestURI inside loop bodies.

PERF-010.description: Parses templates on the request path instead of compiling them once at process start or initialization time.
PERF-010.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match template parsing on request-serving paths and suppress when template initialization is intentionally isolated and cached.
PERF-010.name: Template Parsing On Request Path
PERF-010.original_description: Detect template.New(...).Parse(...), template.ParseFiles(...), or template.Must(template.Parse...) inside handlers or middleware.

PERF-011.description: Allocates a new http.Client on request-serving paths where a shared client should usually be reused.
PERF-011.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match http.Client allocation on hot paths and suppress when a short-lived specialized client is intentionally required and not repeatedly recreated.
PERF-011.name: HTTP Client Allocation Per Request
PERF-011.original_description: Detect http.Client construction inside handlers, middleware, or looped call paths.

PERF-012.description: Creates prepared statements on request-serving paths instead of caching or initializing them once.
PERF-012.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match prepared statement setup on hot paths and suppress when statement creation is amortized, cached, or intentionally scoped.
PERF-012.name: Prepared Statement Setup On Request Path
PERF-012.original_description: Detect db.Prepare or db.PrepareContext inside handlers, middleware, or inner loops.

PERF-013.description: Creates time.After values repeatedly inside long-running loops where reusable timers or tickers would avoid churn.
PERF-013.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match time.After in long-running loops and suppress when the loop is clearly bounded or already uses a reusable timer strategy.
PERF-013.name: time.After Allocation In Long-Running Loop
PERF-013.original_description: Detect time.After(...) inside worker loops, retry loops, or polling loops.

PERF-014.description: Performs repeated filesystem directory scans inside loops when the scanned set is stable and could be hoisted or cached.
PERF-014.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match directory scans inside loops and suppress when the scan target is intentionally dynamic per iteration.
PERF-014.name: Directory Scan Inside Loop
PERF-014.original_description: Detect filepath.Glob, os.ReadDir, or similar directory scans inside loop bodies.

PERF-015.description: Performs repeated numeric-to-string conversion inside loops where buffered writes, cached conversion, or batching would be cheaper.
PERF-015.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match repeated strconv formatting in loops and suppress when the cost is clearly amortized or the loop is not on a hot path.
PERF-015.name: strconv Formatting Inside Loop
PERF-015.original_description: Detect strconv.Itoa, FormatInt, FormatUint, or FormatFloat inside loop bodies.

PERF-016.description: Allocates a fresh bytes.Buffer inside an inner loop instead of reusing a resettable buffer across iterations.
PERF-016.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match buffer allocation in loops and suppress when the allocation is one-shot by design or when reset-based reuse is not valid for the code shape.
PERF-016.name: Buffer Reallocation Inside Loop
PERF-016.original_description: Detect bytes.Buffer{} or new(bytes.Buffer) inside loop bodies when reuse is viable.

PERF-017.description: Builds a string with the + operator inside a loop, causing repeated allocation and copy churn. Use strings.Builder or a pre-sized buffer.
PERF-017.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match string += inside loop bodies and suppress when the loop is bounded and tiny, or when strings.Builder is already used.
PERF-017.name: String Concatenation With Plus Inside Loop
PERF-017.original_description: Detect s = s + x or s += x inside a for or range loop body.

PERF-018.description: Copies a slice header into a function that only needs a sub-range, causing the full backing array to remain live and increase GC pressure.
PERF-018.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match large slice passing patterns and suppress when the callee mutates the slice in place or escapes it.
PERF-018.name: Unnecessary Slice Copy On Reslice
PERF-018.original_description: Detect passing a large slice where a sub-slice expression would suffice, or full copy where append-to-self would work.

PERF-019.description: Iterates over a slice of large structs by value, copying each element. Range by index or pointer to avoid per-iteration copies.
PERF-019.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match range by value over large structs and suppress when the copy is intentional for value semantics.
PERF-019.name: Large Value Copy In Range Loop
PERF-019.original_description: Detect for _, v := range largeStructSlice where the element type is a non-trivial struct.

PERF-020.description: Uses the reflect package on a hot request path. Reflection is significantly slower than typed or codegen-based dispatch.
PERF-020.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match reflect usage on hot paths and suppress when code is initialization-only, codegen, or generic helper that runs once.
PERF-020.name: Reflection Usage In Hot Path
PERF-020.original_description: Detect reflect.ValueOf, reflect.TypeOf, reflect.New, or method lookup via reflect on request-serving paths.

PERF-021.description: Uses io.ReadAll to fully buffer a request body that may be large. This materializes the whole body in memory and can amplify memory pressure.
PERF-021.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match io.ReadAll on request bodies and suppress when bodies are small or already bounded by middleware.
PERF-021.name: io.ReadAll On Large Request Body
PERF-021.original_description: Detect io.ReadAll, ioutil.ReadAll, or io.ReadFull on http.Request.Body in handlers.

PERF-022.description: Reads an entire file inside a request handler on every call. Large or remote files cause repeated disk I/O on a hot path.
PERF-022.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match file reads in handler paths and suppress when file is small config or already cached via sync.Once.
PERF-022.name: os.ReadFile Inside Handler
PERF-022.original_description: Detect os.ReadFile, ioutil.ReadFile, or os.ReadFile in handler or middleware functions.

PERF-023.description: Allocates a new bytes.Reader or bytes.Buffer for each request when a sync.Pool or reusable buffer would be cheaper.
PERF-023.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match per-request buffer allocation and suppress when the buffer is intentionally short-lived or escapes into goroutines.
PERF-023.name: bytes.NewReader Allocation Per Request
PERF-023.original_description: Detect bytes.NewReader, bytes.NewBuffer, or new(bytes.Buffer) on request-serving paths.

PERF-024.description: Creates a new hash instance (sha256, sha1, md5) inside a tight loop where the hasher could be reset or hoisted.
PERF-024.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match per-iteration hasher creation and suppress when the loop is bounded or already uses a pooled hasher.
PERF-024.name: Crypto Hashing In Tight Loop
PERF-024.original_description: Detect sha256.New, sha1.New, md5.New, or hmac.New inside loop bodies or per-row hot paths.

PERF-025.description: Generates RSA keys inside request handlers or per-call paths. RSA key generation is expensive and should be cached.
PERF-025.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match key generation in hot paths and suppress when keys are intentionally rotated or generated only at startup.
PERF-025.name: RSA Key Generation Per Request
PERF-025.original_description: Detect rsa.GenerateKey, rsa.GenerateMultiPrimeKey, or ecdsa.GenerateKey in handlers or middleware.

PERF-026.description: Performs base64 encoding or decoding repeatedly inside a loop. Encoders and decoders can be reused, and streaming is often cheaper.
PERF-026.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match base64 conversion in loops and suppress when input sizes are tiny or the conversion is one-shot.
PERF-026.name: Base64 Encode Or Decode In Loop
PERF-026.original_description: Detect base64.StdEncoding.EncodeToString, DecodeString, NewEncoder, or NewDecoder inside loop bodies.

PERF-027.description: Allocates short-lived buffers or objects on hot paths without using sync.Pool, increasing GC pressure.
PERF-027.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match per-call buffer allocation patterns and suppress when objects escape to other goroutines or are stored beyond the request.
PERF-027.name: Missed sync.Pool Reuse Opportunity
PERF-027.original_description: Detect allocation of bytes.Buffer, []byte, or frequently used structs in hot paths without a sync.Pool wrapper.

PERF-028.description: Embeds or constructs a sync.Mutex per request or per struct instance where a shared mutex or atomic primitive would be cheaper.
PERF-028.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match mutex-per-instance patterns and suppress when per-record locking is the documented concurrency model.
PERF-028.name: Sync Mutex Allocation Per Request
PERF-028.original_description: Detect sync.Mutex{}, sync.RWMutex{}, or pointer-embedded mutexes inside request-scoped or per-record structs.

PERF-029.description: Spawns goroutines without a worker pool, semaphore, or bounded concurrency, which can exhaust memory and file descriptors under load.
PERF-029.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match unconstrained go statements and suppress when the input set is bounded or the goroutine is part of a worker pool.
PERF-029.name: Unbounded Goroutine Spawn
PERF-029.original_description: Detect go func(){} in a loop, in a request handler, or inside a fan-out pattern with no semaphore or worker pool.

PERF-030.description: Creates a new context.Background() inside a goroutine launched from a request handler, breaking cancellation and request-scoped values.
PERF-030.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match context.Background() in goroutine launches and suppress when the context is intentionally detached from the parent request.
PERF-030.name: context.Background In Goroutine From Request
PERF-030.original_description: Detect context.Background() or context.TODO() in goroutines spawned from handlers or middleware.

PERF-031.description: Uses defer in performance-sensitive hot functions where the per-call overhead is measurable. defer has a small but non-zero cost in Go 1.13 and earlier.
PERF-031.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match defer usage in hot paths and suppress when readability outweighs the micro-overhead.
PERF-031.name: Defer In Hot Function
PERF-031.original_description: Detect defer calls in functions that are called in tight loops or on request paths where profiling shows hot frames.

PERF-032.description: Converts between string and []byte repeatedly using []byte(s) or string(b), which copies the underlying data.
PERF-032.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match string/byte conversions in hot paths and suppress when copy is required by API or when unsafe zero-copy is not safe.
PERF-032.name: String Byte Conversion In Hot Path
PERF-032.original_description: Detect []byte(str) or string(byteSlice) inside loop bodies or per-request hot paths.

PERF-033.description: Iterates over a large slice without using indexed access, missing opportunities for early termination or two-pointer patterns.
PERF-033.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match range-over-slice patterns and suppress when iteration is clean enough to justify the readability.
PERF-033.name: Range Over Large Slice With Early Break Missing
PERF-033.original_description: Detect range over large slices in handlers, batch processors, or search code where indexed scan would be more efficient.

PERF-034.description: Appends to a slice inside a map iteration, which is safe but often indicates accidental quadratic copying when the slice grows large.
PERF-034.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match append inside map ranges and suppress when the map is small or preallocation is already correct.
PERF-034.name: Map Iteration With Append
PERF-034.original_description: Detect append inside a for k,v := range map body where the slice grows without preallocation.

PERF-035.description: Passes concrete values into interface{} parameters on hot paths, causing escape-to-heap and dynamic dispatch overhead.
PERF-035.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match interface{} boxing and suppress when the API requires it or when the cost is amortized.
PERF-035.name: Interface Boxing On Hot Path
PERF-035.original_description: Detect fmt.Sprintf with non-string args, or interface{}-typed function parameters on hot paths.

PERF-036.description: Captures a loop variable by reference inside a goroutine (pre-Go 1.22 semantics), causing all goroutines to see the final value.
PERF-036.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match goroutines that capture loop variables and suppress when the variable is correctly copied or the project targets Go 1.22+.
PERF-036.name: Loop Variable Capture In Goroutine
PERF-036.original_description: Detect go func(){ use(v) }() where v is a for/range loop variable without a per-iteration copy or shadowing.

PERF-037.description: Repeatedly appends to a slice without preallocation or with insufficient capacity hint, causing repeated reallocation and copy.
PERF-037.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match append in hot grow patterns and suppress when the final size is unknown or already preallocated.
PERF-037.name: Slice Growth Miscalculation
PERF-037.original_description: Detect append with no make + cap hint in a loop or function that grows a large slice.

PERF-038.description: Uses an unbuffered channel for high-throughput producer/consumer patterns, causing unnecessary blocking and synchronization.
PERF-038.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match unbuffered channels in hot pipelines and suppress when strict synchronization is required by design.
PERF-038.name: Unbuffered Channel In Producer Consumer
PERF-038.original_description: Detect make(chan T) in producer/consumer pipelines where buffered channels would smooth throughput.

PERF-039.description: Uses select with a default case in a tight loop, which becomes a busy-wait pattern that pins a CPU core.
PERF-039.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match select-default in loops and suppress when a small intentional sleep or backoff is also present.
PERF-039.name: Select With Default In Tight Loop
PERF-039.original_description: Detect for { select { case ...; default: ... } } in polling or worker loops.

PERF-040.description: Calls time.Now() repeatedly inside hot paths where a single timestamp at function entry would suffice.
PERF-040.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match multiple time.Now calls and suppress when distinct timestamps are required by the logic.
PERF-040.name: time.Now Calls In Hot Path
PERF-040.original_description: Detect time.Now() called multiple times in the same function body, especially inside loops.

PERF-041.description: Uses the standard log package or log.Println/Printf on production hot paths, where structured, leveled, or async loggers are cheaper.
PERF-041.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match standard log usage in hot paths and suppress when the project intentionally uses the standard log package or the call is gated by verbosity.
PERF-041.name: log Standard Logger In Production Hot Path
PERF-041.original_description: Detect log.Println, log.Printf, log.Print inside handler or loop bodies.

PERF-042.description: Uses fmt.Errorf for error wrapping without format verbs, which is slower than errors.New and requires extra parsing.
PERF-042.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match fmt.Errorf with no verbs and suppress when formatting is added for consistency.
PERF-042.name: fmt.Errorf With No Formatting In Hot Path
PERF-042.original_description: Detect fmt.Errorf("static message") where the string contains no %s, %d, %v, or other format verbs.

PERF-043.description: Recovers from panics in tight loops or hot functions, which adds defer setup and runtime cost on every iteration.
PERF-043.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match defer-recover in hot paths and suppress when the boundary is meaningful or the recover is required for safety.
PERF-043.name: Panic Recovery In Hot Path
PERF-043.original_description: Detect defer func(){ recover() }() inside hot loops or per-request paths.

PERF-044.description: Performs the same type assertion on an interface value multiple times, where a single assertion with reuse would be cheaper.
PERF-044.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match repeated type assertions and suppress when assertion success is the common path that the type system cannot optimize.
PERF-044.name: Repeated Type Assertion On Same Interface
PERF-044.original_description: Detect x.(T) called multiple times for the same value inside a function or loop body.

PERF-045.description: Appends to a slice inside a loop without setting an initial capacity, leading to repeated reallocation as the slice grows geometrically.
PERF-045.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match append-in-loop without preallocation and suppress when the final size is genuinely unknown.
PERF-045.name: Append Without Capacity Hint In Loop
PERF-045.original_description: Detect append in a for/range loop where the slice has no make([]T, 0, hint) before the loop.

PERF-046.description: Uses strings.TrimSpace or similar trimming functions on hot paths where the result is the same as the input, allocating a new string.
PERF-046.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match string trimming in hot paths and suppress when correctness requires trimming.
PERF-046.name: String Trimming With Allocations
PERF-046.original_description: Detect strings.TrimSpace, Trim, TrimPrefix, TrimSuffix in hot paths without checking the need first.

PERF-047.description: Uses strings.Split or strings.SplitN in hot paths where a streaming scanner or precomputed index would be cheaper.
PERF-047.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match strings.Split in hot paths and suppress when input is small or splitting semantics are required.
PERF-047.name: strings.Split In Hot Path
PERF-047.original_description: Detect strings.Split, strings.SplitN, or strings.SplitAfter inside hot loops or per-request parsing.

PERF-048.description: Compares long byte slices or strings without short-circuit optimizations, where length or hash precheck would help.
PERF-048.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match byte/string equality in hot paths and suppress when the full compare is required for correctness.
PERF-048.name: bytes.Equal On Long Strings
PERF-048.original_description: Detect bytes.Equal or strings.EqualFold on long inputs in hot paths without an early length or prefix check.

PERF-049.description: Uses copy(dst, src) where the destination length is not checked, leading to silent truncation or wasted capacity.
PERF-049.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match copy calls in hot paths and suppress when lengths are explicitly validated upstream.
PERF-049.name: Copy With Mismatched Length
PERF-049.original_description: Detect copy(dst, src) where dst was allocated with a wrong cap or where bounds are not validated.

PERF-050.description: Calls regexp.MatchString (or similar regex match helpers) inside a loop instead of compiling once and reusing the pattern.
PERF-050.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match regexp.MatchString in loops and suppress when the pattern is genuinely dynamic per iteration.
PERF-050.name: regexp.MatchString In Loop
PERF-050.original_description: Detect regexp.MatchString or regexp.Match inside loop bodies or per-row processing.
